scenario: A process should start whenever an inbound webhook receives a delivery-notification from a logistics partner. The team configures an Inbound Webhook Connector wired to a Start Event.
question: Which Start Event variant fits?

options.text: None Start Event. A None Start Event triggers only when a process instance is created explicitly via the Camunda 8 REST API call CreateProcessInstance. It has no built-in subscription mechanism for external events or webhook payloads. Connecting an Inbound Webhook Connector to a None Start Event does not give it event-triggered behaviour; it would still require an explicit API call to start.

Message Start Event configured with the Inbound Webhook Connector as its trigger. When a webhook HTTP request arrives and matches the connector's configured pattern, the connector publishes a BPMN message to Zeebe. Zeebe matches the message name against the Message Start Event subscription and creates a new process instance, carrying the webhook payload as process variables automatically.

Timer Start Event. A Timer Start Event creates process instances on a scheduled basis -- at a fixed time, after a duration, or according to a cron expression. It has no mechanism to react to an inbound webhook HTTP request from an external partner. A schedule-based start is fundamentally different from an event-driven start triggered by an incoming logistics notification message.
Conditional Start Event. The canonical pairing for an external webhook trigger is a Message Start Event with an Inbound Webhook Connector.

a.text: Incorrect. Message Start Event is the canonical trigger for externally-initiated processes. Combined with an Inbound Webhook Connector, the webhook hit publishes the message and Zeebe creates a new instance.

b.text: Correct. None Start fires only on explicit CreateProcessInstance API call, not on events.

c.text: Incorrect. Timer fires on schedule, not on incoming requests.

d.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 does support Conditional Start Events (per bpmn/conditional-events/conditional-events.md:23-33 -- implemented for execution; new instance created when FEEL condition evaluates true), but they fire on process-variable state changes (or are triggered via the Orchestration Cluster REST API), not on external HTTP webhook calls. For webhook-initiated processes, Message Start Event + Webhook Connector is the canonical pairing.

explanation: None Start fires only on explicit CreateProcessInstance API call, not on events.
